📚 KPI Definitions & Equations

MTBF — Mean Time Between Failures

MTBF = Total Operating Days ÷ Number of Failures

Average time between consecutive failure events across the fleet. Includes censored (running) wells in total days. Target: ≥1,200 days.

MTTF — Mean Time To Failure

MTTF = Sum of Run Life (failed units only) ÷ Number of Failed Units

Average run life of failed ESPs only. Does NOT include running wells. Target: ≥900 days.

AFRL — Average Failed Run Life

AFRL = Total Run Life (failed) ÷ Count of Failed Units

Equivalent to MTTF in this system. Measures how long ESPs operated before failing. Used in reliability trend analysis.

Overall Average Run Life

Avg RL = Total Run Life (all wells) ÷ Total Well Runs

Includes both failed and running (censored) units. Running units contribute their current run life to the average.

Failure Index (per 100 units)

FI = (Failures ÷ Total Installed) × 100

Normalized failure rate per 100 installations. Industry benchmark: <15 per 100 per year (C-FER standard).

EFR — Early Failure Rate

EFR = (Failures ÷ Total Installed) × 100%

Percentage of installed ESPs that have failed. Monitors fleet-wide reliability status at a given point in time.

Reliability Index

RI = (Running Units ÷ Total Installed) × 100%

Percentage of fleet currently operational. Complement to failure index. Target: ≥85%.

Infant Failure Rate (≤30d)

IFR = (Failures ≤30d ÷ Total Failures) × 100%

Proportion of failures within 30 days of startup. Indicates installation quality, commissioning process, or equipment defects. Target: <5%.

Survival Probability — Kaplan-Meier

S(t) = ∏ [(n − d) ÷ n] for all failure events ≤ t

Non-parametric probability that an ESP survives beyond time t. Correctly handles censored (running) units. 95% CI via Greenwood SE. Ref: NIST Handbook 151.

Failure Cause Data Sources

Cause = cfer_rfp_general → failure_reason → "Unclassified"

Qarun (Egypt) records use C-FER Rev 4.6 taxonomy. Saudi Aramco (MNIF/SFNY) records use free-text failure_reason field. Charts automatically use whichever is available, so no data is hidden when switching between countries.

Global Filter

Country → Customer → Field → Vendor (cascading)

The global filter bar (below the top navigation) applies to ALL tabs simultaneously. Use "Apply to All" to propagate the selection. Individual tab filters apply on top of the global filter. Year range filters by failure date for failed wells, install date for running wells.

ℹ️ Methodology
Kaplan-Meier (KM) Estimator — Non-parametric method per NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook of Statistical Methods.
R(tᵢ) = R(tᵢ₋₁) × (nᵢ - dᵢ) / nᵢ  |  where nᵢ = units at risk, dᵢ = failures at time tᵢ
Censored observations (currently running wells) are included at their current run life and removed after their censoring time.
SE = R(t) × √[Σ dᵢ/(nᵢ(nᵢ-dᵢ))]  (Greenwood's formula)
MTTF = area under survival curve (trapezoidal integration)
